parameters.query:
UPDATE document_sequences
SET
  last_number = last_number + 1,
  updated_at  = NOW()
WHERE document_type = '{{document_type}}'
  AND year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW())::int
RETURNING
  document_type,
  prefix,
  year,
  last_number,
  prefix || '-' || year::text || '-' || LPAD(last_number::text, 3, '0') AS document_number;

parameters.noteText:
UTIL — Document Number Generator

Generates the next sequential document number for any document type.

INPUT:
  document_type   TEXT   One of:
                           invoice          → DEE-INV-2026-NNN
                           proposal         → DEE-PRO-2026-NNN
                           contract         → DEE-CON-2026-NNN
                           nda              → DEE-NDA-2026-NNN
                           onboarding       → DEE-ONB-2026-NNN
                           report           → DEE-RPT-2026-NNN
                           quote            → DEE-QUO-2026-NNN
                           receipt          → DEE-REC-2026-NNN
                           team_onboarding  → DEE-EMP-2026-NNN

OUTPUT (from RETURNING clause):
  document_type    invoice
  prefix           DEE-INV
  year             2026
  last_number      1
  document_number  DEE-INV-2026-001   ← use this

HOW IT WORKS:
  Single atomic UPDATE + RETURNING on document_sequences.
  Increments last_number and returns the new formatted number
  in one database round-trip. Concurrent-safe — no two workflow
  executions can receive the same number.

CALLED BY:
  GENERATE-invoice    → document_type = "invoice"
  GENERATE-proposal   → document_type = "proposal"
  GENERATE-contract   → document_type = "contract"
  GENERATE-nda        → document_type = "nda"

YEAR ROLLOVER:
  The WHERE clause uses EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW()) so it automatically
  uses the correct year row. On Jan 1 a new row must exist for the
  new year — handled by the year-end archive workflow.

Document number format:
  {PREFIX}-{YEAR}-{NNN}

  NNN = zero-padded to 3 digits (LPAD 3).
  Upgrades to 4 digits automatically once NNN > 999
  (LPAD only pads — does not truncate).

Examples:
  DEE-INV-2026-001   first invoice of 2026
  DEE-INV-2026-042   42nd invoice of 2026
  DEE-PRO-2026-007   7th proposal of 2026
  DEE-CON-2026-003   3rd contract of 2026
  DEE-NDA-2026-001   first NDA of 2026

Storage path (GCS) convention:
  invoices/2026/DEE-INV-2026-001.pdf
  proposals/2026/DEE-PRO-2026-007.pdf
  contracts/2026/DEE-CON-2026-003.pdf

The generation pipelines use document_number as both:
  1. The documents.document_number value (DB record)
  2. The PDF filename (storage_key)
